title: Device lifecycle: start/stop/restart/self_test
content:
Every rack device inherits from BaseDevice and implements four lifecycle methods:
- start(): spawn background threads, open connections, register with rack
- stop(): signal threads, close connections, post DEVICE_STOPPED to channel
- restart(): stop() then start(); guaranteed clean state
- self_test(): exercise the device's critical path, return pass/fail with reason

The shim calls these; devices never call each other's lifecycle methods directly.
Pattern: shim.start() → device.start(); shim.stop() → device.stop().
Lifecycle events are logged at INFO level and posted to the shared channel.

title: Sprint ticket shape: description sections
content:
Every sprint ticket description contains these labeled sections:

<1-3 sentences: problem and proposed shape>

**Affected files:** <comma-separated paths, or 'TBD'>
**Design rules:** <which architecture rules apply; 'none apply' after thinking about it>
**Scope boundary:** <what is IN; what is explicitly OUT>
**Completion criteria:** <machine-verifiable done condition>

Completion criteria is the answer to 'how do we know we're done?' asked at the START of the ticket, not the end. Every ticket has one.

title: Channel event format: VERB|key=val|key=val
content:
Channel events use pipe-delimited key=value format:
  GRANNY_DISPATCH|ticket=T-foo|worker=claude|size=S|tags=Platform
  MINION_RESULT|ticket=T-foo|signal=DONE|tier=analyst|cost_usd=0.0012

Rules:
- First token is the event VERB in SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE
- Remaining tokens are key=value pairs
- No spaces inside tokens (use underscores)
- ticket= always present when event is ticket-scoped
- Log at INFO; post via post_to_channel(msg, author=device_name, channel=channel_name)

title: Availability semaphore: worker_id.available.true/.false files
content:
Worker availability is signaled via files in ~/.granny/available/:
- {worker_id}.available.true  — worker opts in to receive dispatches
- {worker_id}.available.false — worker explicitly blocks all dispatches

Evaluation: .false wins over .true; neither file = unavailable.
use is_available(worker_id) from devices/granny/availability.py.

For graceful shutdown: mark_unavailable(worker_id) before stopping.
For startup: mark_available(worker_id) after passing self_test().
Semaphore state survives process restart (files persist on disk).

title: KnightlyBuilder principle: externalize all daemon state
content:
Every device/daemon must externalize ALL runtime state so it can restart freely at any time without data loss.

External state tiers:
- Files (flat JSON): ephemeral cycle-local data (e.g. dispatched ticket IDs this cycle)
- Postgres: durable state (tickets, memories, pattern records)
- Channel posts: stats and lifecycle events (DEVICE_STATS|uptime=...|dispatched=...)

Test: 'what happens if this process restarts right now?' If the answer is 'state is lost', externalize it.

Provenance: KnightlyBuilder distributed build system (pre-Jenkins), rebuilt 3× across SilkTest/VB/Java. External state = maintenance freedom; internal state = restart penalty.
